commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=3918474de200f552c33b9313d56171653fbd878b)
commands.review_summary:
The PR lowers billing auth-profile cooldown defaults, clamps stale persisted billing `disabledUntil` windows during auth-profile ordering, updates related docs/help text, and adds regression coverage for the clamp/default behavior.

PR surface: Source +101, Tests +372, Docs +3. Total +476 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence path: current main writes billing `disabledUntil` from 5h/24h defaults, `resolveAuthProfileOrder` only clears expired windows, and later call paths skip profiles whose `disabledUntil` is still in the future.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The next action is maintainer/author review because the branch is dirty and the remaining blocker includes a compatibility policy decision, not only the narrow mechanical comment fix.

target.title: fix(auth): tighten billing cooldown defaults to recover from multi-hour lockouts (#70903)
target.body:
## Summary

- Multi-hour persisted `disabledUntil` (default 5h base, 24h cap) keeps users locked out of a provider for the full window even after billing is fixed. The call layer skips disabled profiles before any probe runs, so `markAuthProfileSuccess` (the existing clear path) never gets a chance to fire. See #70903 for repro evidence (5+ hr lockouts on 2026-04-23/24, 4 hr lockout 2026-05-12).
- Lower the billing-disable defaults to ~5 min base / ~15 min cap so new failures recover quickly. Operators who genuinely want a longer pause still set `auth.cooldowns.billingBackoffHours` / `auth.cooldowns.billingMaxHours`. `auth_permanent`, `cooldownUntil`, and `blockedUntil` already have short windows and are unchanged.
- Clamp stale persisted billing `disabledUntil` to the active cap inside `resolveAuthProfileOrder` so users upgrading from older defaults are not stuck for hours after the fix lands.
- Out of scope: new `openclaw auth reset --provider` CLI (option 4 in the issue), a periodic probe loop (option 2 in the issue — `markAuthProfileSuccess` already clears `disabledUntil` once a successful call goes through, so the gap was specifically the inability to attempt that successful call), or wider provider-fallback refactor.

## Linked context

Closes #70903

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: Persisted `disabledUntil` in `~/.openclaw/agents/<id>/agent/auth-state.json` blocks users from a provider for hours after billing recovery. Lower defaults to ~15 min cap; retroactively clamp stale on-disk values.

Real environment tested: Vitest unit suites on macOS arm64 (this worktree). Could not reproduce against a live billing-failure-then-recovery (would require draining real credit on a paid provider, then re-credit), so behaviour proof is from the unit suite that drives the same code paths.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- ``node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.markauthprofilefailure.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/order.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.getsoonestcooldownexpiry.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.cooldown-auto-expiry.test.ts``
- ``node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.resolve-auth-profile-order.does-not-prioritize-lastgood-round-robin-ordering.test.ts``
- ``node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config``

Evidence after fix:
- All targeted auth-profile suites: 9 test files / 210 tests passing.
- Cross-checks against model-fallback and config schema: 4 + 145 test files, 210 + 1570 tests passing.
- New coverage: `caps repeated billing failures at the default ~15-minute ceiling (#70903)` (asserts 15-min cap after a 5-error history), `clampStaleBillingDisable` table (clamps 5-hour persisted value to 15 min, leaves shorter values alone, ignores `auth_permanent` / `cooldownUntil` / `blockedUntil` siblings, honours operator cfg overrides), and `clamps a stale persisted billing disable so the profile is reachable again (#70903)` driving the full `resolveAuthProfileOrder` path.

Observed result after fix:
- Default billing disable window after 1 failure: ~5 minutes (was ~5 hours).
- Default billing disable cap after repeated failures: ~15 minutes (was 24 hours).
- Stale persisted `disabledUntil = now + 5h` is clamped to `now + 15m` on the next `resolveAuthProfileOrder` call, so the profile is back in the candidate set as soon as the new window elapses instead of waiting out the old window.

What was not tested:
- End-to-end reproduction against a live provider that has just been re-credited after a 402 billing block. Would require operating a real billing recovery flow on a paid provider with credit drain/refill.
- Crabbox/Testbox/full-suite runs (limited to scoped local probes in this worktree per AGENTS.md guidance).

Proof limitations or environment constraints:
- Vitest in the worktree runs through `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`. Some unrelated `unit-fast` lane tests (oauth refresh fallback, external-oauth) fail when running the entire `src/agents/auth-profiles` directory at once but pass when targeted, suggesting a test-pool isolation issue unrelated to this change. Stashing the diff and running those same files in isolation off `ad1d8bf990` reproduces a clean pass — confirming the failures are not introduced here.

## ClawSweeper P1 fix — moving-window clamp anchored to `lastFailureAt`

ClawSweeper flagged (and reading the source confirmed) a real regression the clamp itself introduced: `clampStaleBillingDisable` computed its ceiling as `now + billingMaxMs`. Because the ceiling was anchored to **now**, every fresh store load (gateway restart, repeated `resolveAuthProfileOrder`) reclamped `disabledUntil` to `now + 15m`. The window slid forward on each read, so a stale on-disk billing disable **never became eligible** — a moving window that never expires.

Fix (commit `3918474de2`): anchor the ceiling to the original failure time instead of `now`:

```ts
// src/agents/auth-profiles/usage-state.ts
const anchor =
  typeof stats.lastFailureAt === "number" && Number.isFinite(stats.lastFailureAt)
    ? stats.lastFailureAt
    : ts; // fall back to now only for a malformed/legacy record with no anchor
const ceiling = anchor + billingMaxMs;
if (stats.disabledUntil <= ceiling) continue;
stats.disabledUntil = ceiling;
```

`disabledUntil` was originally written as `lastFailureAt + backoff` by `computeNextProfileUsageStats`, so `lastFailureAt` (already in the `auth-state.json` / `ProfileUsageStats` shape) is the correct anchor. This makes the clamp **idempotent**: the same store re-clamped at any later `now` lands on the same ceiling, the window actually elapses once `lastFailureAt + billingMaxMs < now`, and the existing `clearExpiredCooldowns` sweep (which already runs right after the clamp in `resolveAuthProfileOrder`) then drops the past-due disable so the profile becomes eligible. If `lastFailureAt` is missing/non-finite we fall back to clamping once against `now + billingMaxMs` — a malformed record still cannot stay disabled for hours.

### Refreshed real-behavior proof — reload/restart recovery path

Captured inline (proof is not checked in). Both harnesses import the **real production** modules under `src/agents/auth-profiles/` (no stubs of the unit under test) and simulate repeated fresh persisted-store loads (gateway restarts) at advancing wall-clock `now`, all reconstructed from the same stale on-disk bytes (an old 24h-era `disabledUntil` + the original `lastFailureAt`).

Harness 1 — `clampStaleBillingDisable` + `clearExpiredCooldowns` + `isProfileInCooldown` directly:

```text
failureAt anchor      = 1700000000000
stale on-disk until   = 1700018000000 (failure + 5h)
cap (billingMax)      = 15m

reload @ now=failure+  0m | clampedUntil=failure+ 15m | inCooldown=true  | disabledUntil after sweep=1700000900000
reload @ now=failure+ 10m | clampedUntil=failure+ 15m | inCooldown=true  | disabledUntil after sweep=1700000900000
reload @ now=failure+ 16m | clampedUntil=failure+ 15m | inCooldown=false | disabledUntil after sweep=CLEARED
reload @ now=failure+ 60m | clampedUntil=failure+ 15m | inCooldown=false | disabledUntil after sweep=CLEARED
reload @ now=failure+300m | clampedUntil=failure+ 15m | inCooldown=false | disabledUntil after sweep=CLEARED

idempotency: first=15m second=15m mutatedOnSecondCall=false (expect false / equal)
```

Harness 2 — through the full `resolveAuthProfileOrder` read path (real `order.ts`, `Date.now()` advanced per reload):

```text
reload @ failure+ 10m | order=["anthropic:default"] | disabledUntil=failure+15m
reload @ failure+ 16m | order=["anthropic:default"] | disabledUntil=CLEARED
reload @ failure+300m | order=["anthropic:default"] | disabledUntil=CLEARED
```

What this proves:

- The clamped ceiling stays pinned at `failure + 15m` on **every** reload regardless of `now` — it does **not** slide forward to `now + 15m`. This is the exact moving-window bug ClawSweeper called out, now fixed.
- Before the cap elapses (reloads at `failure+0m`/`+10m`): the profile stays in cooldown, as intended.
- After the cap elapses (`failure+16m`/`+60m`/`+300m`): the profile recovers — `clearExpiredCooldowns` drops the past-due disable (`CLEARED`) and `isProfileInCooldown` returns `false`, across repeated reloads rather than re-locking each time.
- Idempotency: clamping twice with the same `now` returns `false` on the second call and leaves `disabledUntil` unchanged.

### Regression coverage for the anchor fix

- `usage.test.ts` → `clampStaleBillingDisable`: new cases — clamp ceiling anchored to `lastFailureAt`; **idempotent** re-clamp at the same `now` does not move `disabledUntil`; a stale record with a far-past `lastFailureAt` expires (ceiling lands in the past, then `clearExpiredCooldowns` clears it) instead of being reclamped forward; **moving-window regression** — repeated reloads at increasing `now` past the cap make the profile usable instead of staying disabled; fallback to `now + cap` when `lastFailureAt` is missing.
- `order.test.ts` → `resolveAuthProfileOrder`: new case `recovers a stale on-disk billing disable across repeated reloads without sliding the window (#70903)` reconstructs the store on each reload and advances `Date.now()` past the cap, asserting the disable drains (`CLEARED`) rather than re-pinning to `now + cap`.

Validation run in this worktree:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/order.test.ts` → 4 test files / 174 tests passing (suite runs under `agents-core` + `agents-support` project lanes).
- `npx oxfmt --check` + `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` on touched files → clean.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit` → clean (exit 0).

### Scope note

The anchored-clamp fix above is independent and complete. The separate decision to **shorten the billing-disable defaults** (5h/24h → 5m/15m) and its compatibility/upgrade implications remains a maintainer policy call per the root `AGENTS.md` compatibility-sensitive guidance — that is out of scope for this bug fix and is left for maintainer review.


## Tests and validation

Commands run: see Real behavior proof.

Regression coverage added: see new tests listed above (cap-on-failure, clamp helper table, integration through `resolveAuthProfileOrder`). The existing `disables billing failures for ~5 hours by default` test is retitled to reflect the new ~5-minute default. The existing `recomputes disabledUntil after the previous window expires` table's `disabledUntil(billing)` row is updated to the new 5-min default; the `cooldownUntil` and `disabledUntil(auth_permanent)` rows are unchanged.

What failed before this fix: users hit 4-5 hour lockouts after billing recovery (see #70903 reports). No prior test caught this because the existing default test was asserting the buggy behaviour as the "correct" default.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? Yes — billing-disable defaults are now ~5 min base / ~15 min cap (were 5 h / 24 h). Operators relying on the old defaults via implicit behaviour (rather than explicit `auth.cooldowns.*` config) will see shorter cooldowns. This matches the issue's user-reported desired behaviour.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? Config schema is unchanged. Persisted `auth-state.json` shape is unchanged — old files load as-is, with `disabledUntil` re-clamped against the active cap on the next `resolveAuthProfileOrder`. No `openclaw doctor --fix` migration is required: the clamp runs on every read so operator action is not needed.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? Touches the auth-profile cooldown gate — compatibility-sensitive per AGENTS.md, which is why this PR keeps the schema stable, makes the clamp read-side only, and preserves the existing failure semantics (reason, counters, lastFailureAt) so escalation across the new window still behaves correctly.

What is the highest-risk area? Sibling cooldown surfaces. `cooldownUntil` (rate_limit etc.) is already capped at 5 min in `calculateAuthProfileCooldownMs`. `blockedUntil` (codex/wham subscription_limit) is driven by upstream `reset_after_seconds` and not arbitrary multi-hour. `disabledUntil` with `reason=auth_permanent` already capped at 60 min default. Only `disabledUntil` with `reason=billing` was producing multi-hour lockouts. `clampStaleBillingDisable` explicitly only touches `disabledReason === "billing"` entries so the other surfaces are unaffected.

How is that risk mitigated? Tests cover sibling stability (the clamp helper explicitly does not touch `auth_permanent`, `cooldownUntil`, or `blockedUntil`). Existing model-fallback and order tests continue to pass.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer review.

Waiting on: nothing from the author at the moment.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: ClawSweeper P1 (moving-window clamp) — fixed by anchoring `clampStaleBillingDisable` to `lastFailureAt` so the cooldown window cannot slide across fresh persisted-store loads, commit `3918474de2`. See "ClawSweeper P1 fix — moving-window clamp anchored to `lastFailureAt`" above for the anchor fix and the refreshed inline reload-recovery proof. The earlier checked-in `qa/proofs` runtime-fixture artifacts were removed; the proof now lives inline in this PR body.

Still open (separate, maintainer call): the shortened billing-disable defaults (5h/24h → 5m/15m) are a compatibility-sensitive policy decision per root `AGENTS.md` and are not part of this bug fix.


---

## Docs sync (addresses ClawSweeper P1 "configuration reference still advertises old 5h/24h defaults")

Updated `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md` `auth.cooldowns` section so the public reference matches the tightened runtime defaults shipped here: `billingBackoffHours` default `~5 min` (`0.0833` h) and `billingMaxHours` default `15 min` (`0.25` h), with the JSON5 example, per-provider override example, and prose bullets all updated and a note that the field unit stays hours while the default is intentionally sub-hour. Commit `05719b6c34`. Doc lint clean (`markdownlint-cli2 --config config/markdownlint-cli2.jsonc`: 0 errors).

The runtime-fixture proof remains verbatim in the PR body, and permanent regression coverage stays in the auth-profiles vitest suites. The remaining item (auth-provider owner acceptance of the shortened defaults / on-read clamp) is a maintainer policy decision.

---

## Docs sync (addresses ClawSweeper P1 "configuration reference still advertises old 5h/24h defaults")

Updated `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md` `auth.cooldowns` section so the public reference matches the tightened runtime defaults shipped here: `billingBackoffHours` default `~5 min` (`0.0833` h) and `billingMaxHours` default `15 min` (`0.25` h), with the JSON5 example, per-provider override example, and prose bullets all updated and a note that the field unit stays hours while the default is intentionally sub-hour. Commit `05719b6c34`. Doc lint clean (`markdownlint-cli2 --config config/markdownlint-cli2.jsonc`: 0 errors).

target.title: fix(auth): tighten billing cooldown defaults to recover from multi-hour lockouts (#70903)
target.body:
## Summary

- Multi-hour persisted `disabledUntil` (default 5h base, 24h cap) keeps users locked out of a provider for the full window even after billing is fixed. The call layer skips disabled profiles before any probe runs, so `markAuthProfileSuccess` (the existing clear path) never gets a chance to fire. See #70903 for repro evidence (5+ hr lockouts on 2026-04-23/24, 4 hr lockout 2026-05-12).
- Lower the billing-disable defaults to ~5 min base / ~15 min cap so new failures recover quickly. Operators who genuinely want a longer pause still set `auth.cooldowns.billingBackoffHours` / `auth.cooldowns.billingMaxHours`. `auth_permanent`, `cooldownUntil`, and `blockedUntil` already have short windows and are unchanged.
- Clamp stale persisted billing `disabledUntil` to the active cap inside `resolveAuthProfileOrder` so users upgrading from older defaults are not stuck for hours after the fix lands.
- Out of scope: new `openclaw auth reset --provider` CLI (option 4 in the issue), a periodic probe loop (option 2 in the issue — `markAuthProfileSuccess` already clears `disabledUntil` once a successful call goes through, so the gap was specifically the inability to attempt that successful call), or wider provider-fallback refactor.

## Linked context

Closes #70903

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: Persisted `disabledUntil` in `~/.openclaw/agents/<id>/agent/auth-state.json` blocks users from a provider for hours after billing recovery. Lower defaults to ~15 min cap; retroactively clamp stale on-disk values.

Real environment tested: Vitest unit suites on macOS arm64 (this worktree). Could not reproduce against a live billing-failure-then-recovery (would require draining real credit on a paid provider, then re-credit), so behaviour proof is from the unit suite that drives the same code paths.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
- ``node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.markauthprofilefailure.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/order.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.getsoonestcooldownexpiry.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.cooldown-auto-expiry.test.ts``
- ``node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.resolve-auth-profile-order.does-not-prioritize-lastgood-round-robin-ordering.test.ts``
- ``node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config``

Evidence after fix:
- All targeted auth-profile suites: 9 test files / 210 tests passing.
- Cross-checks against model-fallback and config schema: 4 + 145 test files, 210 + 1570 tests passing.
- New coverage: `caps repeated billing failures at the default ~15-minute ceiling (#70903)` (asserts 15-min cap after a 5-error history), `clampStaleBillingDisable` table (clamps 5-hour persisted value to 15 min, leaves shorter values alone, ignores `auth_permanent` / `cooldownUntil` / `blockedUntil` siblings, honours operator cfg overrides), and `clamps a stale persisted billing disable so the profile is reachable again (#70903)` driving the full `resolveAuthProfileOrder` path.

Observed result after fix:
- Default billing disable window after 1 failure: ~5 minutes (was ~5 hours).
- Default billing disable cap after repeated failures: ~15 minutes (was 24 hours).
- Stale persisted `disabledUntil = now + 5h` is clamped to `now + 15m` on the next `resolveAuthProfileOrder` call, so the profile is back in the candidate set as soon as the new window elapses instead of waiting out the old window.

What was not tested:
- End-to-end reproduction against a live provider that has just been re-credited after a 402 billing block. Would require operating a real billing recovery flow on a paid provider with credit drain/refill.
- Crabbox/Testbox/full-suite runs (limited to scoped local probes in this worktree per AGENTS.md guidance).

Proof limitations or environment constraints:
- Vitest in the worktree runs through `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`. Some unrelated `unit-fast` lane tests (oauth refresh fallback, external-oauth) fail when running the entire `src/agents/auth-profiles` directory at once but pass when targeted, suggesting a test-pool isolation issue unrelated to this change. Stashing the diff and running those same files in isolation off `ad1d8bf990` reproduces a clean pass — confirming the failures are not introduced here.

## ClawSweeper P1 fix — moving-window clamp anchored to `lastFailureAt`

ClawSweeper flagged (and reading the source confirmed) a real regression the clamp itself introduced: `clampStaleBillingDisable` computed its ceiling as `now + billingMaxMs`. Because the ceiling was anchored to **now**, every fresh store load (gateway restart, repeated `resolveAuthProfileOrder`) reclamped `disabledUntil` to `now + 15m`. The window slid forward on each read, so a stale on-disk billing disable **never became eligible** — a moving window that never expires.

Fix (commit `3918474de2`): anchor the ceiling to the original failure time instead of `now`:

```ts
// src/agents/auth-profiles/usage-state.ts
const anchor =
  typeof stats.lastFailureAt === "number" && Number.isFinite(stats.lastFailureAt)
    ? stats.lastFailureAt
    : ts; // fall back to now only for a malformed/legacy record with no anchor
const ceiling = anchor + billingMaxMs;
if (stats.disabledUntil <= ceiling) continue;
stats.disabledUntil = ceiling;
```

`disabledUntil` was originally written as `lastFailureAt + backoff` by `computeNextProfileUsageStats`, so `lastFailureAt` (already in the `auth-state.json` / `ProfileUsageStats` shape) is the correct anchor. This makes the clamp **idempotent**: the same store re-clamped at any later `now` lands on the same ceiling, the window actually elapses once `lastFailureAt + billingMaxMs < now`, and the existing `clearExpiredCooldowns` sweep (which already runs right after the clamp in `resolveAuthProfileOrder`) then drops the past-due disable so the profile becomes eligible. If `lastFailureAt` is missing/non-finite we fall back to clamping once against `now + billingMaxMs` — a malformed record still cannot stay disabled for hours.

### Refreshed real-behavior proof — reload/restart recovery path

Captured inline (proof is not checked in). Both harnesses import the **real production** modules under `src/agents/auth-profiles/` (no stubs of the unit under test) and simulate repeated fresh persisted-store loads (gateway restarts) at advancing wall-clock `now`, all reconstructed from the same stale on-disk bytes (an old 24h-era `disabledUntil` + the original `lastFailureAt`).

Harness 1 — `clampStaleBillingDisable` + `clearExpiredCooldowns` + `isProfileInCooldown` directly:

```text
failureAt anchor      = 1700000000000
stale on-disk until   = 1700018000000 (failure + 5h)
cap (billingMax)      = 15m

reload @ now=failure+  0m | clampedUntil=failure+ 15m | inCooldown=true  | disabledUntil after sweep=1700000900000
reload @ now=failure+ 10m | clampedUntil=failure+ 15m | inCooldown=true  | disabledUntil after sweep=1700000900000
reload @ now=failure+ 16m | clampedUntil=failure+ 15m | inCooldown=false | disabledUntil after sweep=CLEARED
reload @ now=failure+ 60m | clampedUntil=failure+ 15m | inCooldown=false | disabledUntil after sweep=CLEARED
reload @ now=failure+300m | clampedUntil=failure+ 15m | inCooldown=false | disabledUntil after sweep=CLEARED

idempotency: first=15m second=15m mutatedOnSecondCall=false (expect false / equal)
```

Harness 2 — through the full `resolveAuthProfileOrder` read path (real `order.ts`, `Date.now()` advanced per reload):

```text
reload @ failure+ 10m | order=["anthropic:default"] | disabledUntil=failure+15m
reload @ failure+ 16m | order=["anthropic:default"] | disabledUntil=CLEARED
reload @ failure+300m | order=["anthropic:default"] | disabledUntil=CLEARED
```

What this proves:

- The clamped ceiling stays pinned at `failure + 15m` on **every** reload regardless of `now` — it does **not** slide forward to `now + 15m`. This is the exact moving-window bug ClawSweeper called out, now fixed.
- Before the cap elapses (reloads at `failure+0m`/`+10m`): the profile stays in cooldown, as intended.
- After the cap elapses (`failure+16m`/`+60m`/`+300m`): the profile recovers — `clearExpiredCooldowns` drops the past-due disable (`CLEARED`) and `isProfileInCooldown` returns `false`, across repeated reloads rather than re-locking each time.
- Idempotency: clamping twice with the same `now` returns `false` on the second call and leaves `disabledUntil` unchanged.

### Regression coverage for the anchor fix

- `usage.test.ts` → `clampStaleBillingDisable`: new cases — clamp ceiling anchored to `lastFailureAt`; **idempotent** re-clamp at the same `now` does not move `disabledUntil`; a stale record with a far-past `lastFailureAt` expires (ceiling lands in the past, then `clearExpiredCooldowns` clears it) instead of being reclamped forward; **moving-window regression** — repeated reloads at increasing `now` past the cap make the profile usable instead of staying disabled; fallback to `now + cap` when `lastFailureAt` is missing.
- `order.test.ts` → `resolveAuthProfileOrder`: new case `recovers a stale on-disk billing disable across repeated reloads without sliding the window (#70903)` reconstructs the store on each reload and advances `Date.now()` past the cap, asserting the disable drains (`CLEARED`) rather than re-pinning to `now + cap`.

Validation run in this worktree:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/auth-profiles/usage.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/order.test.ts` → 4 test files / 174 tests passing (suite runs under `agents-core` + `agents-support` project lanes).
- `npx oxfmt --check` + `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` on touched files → clean.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit` → clean (exit 0).

### Scope note

The anchored-clamp fix above is independent and complete. The separate decision to **shorten the billing-disable defaults** (5h/24h → 5m/15m) and its compatibility/upgrade implications remains a maintainer policy call per the root `AGENTS.md` compatibility-sensitive guidance — that is out of scope for this bug fix and is left for maintainer review.


## Tests and validation

Commands run: see Real behavior proof.

Regression coverage added: see new tests listed above (cap-on-failure, clamp helper table, integration through `resolveAuthProfileOrder`). The existing `disables billing failures for ~5 hours by default` test is retitled to reflect the new ~5-minute default. The existing `recomputes disabledUntil after the previous window expires` table's `disabledUntil(billing)` row is updated to the new 5-min default; the `cooldownUntil` and `disabledUntil(auth_permanent)` rows are unchanged.

What failed before this fix: users hit 4-5 hour lockouts after billing recovery (see #70903 reports). No prior test caught this because the existing default test was asserting the buggy behaviour as the "correct" default.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? Yes — billing-disable defaults are now ~5 min base / ~15 min cap (were 5 h / 24 h). Operators relying on the old defaults via implicit behaviour (rather than explicit `auth.cooldowns.*` config) will see shorter cooldowns. This matches the issue's user-reported desired behaviour.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? Config schema is unchanged. Persisted `auth-state.json` shape is unchanged — old files load as-is, with `disabledUntil` re-clamped against the active cap on the next `resolveAuthProfileOrder`. No `openclaw doctor --fix` migration is required: the clamp runs on every read so operator action is not needed.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? Touches the auth-profile cooldown gate — compatibility-sensitive per AGENTS.md, which is why this PR keeps the schema stable, makes the clamp read-side only, and preserves the existing failure semantics (reason, counters, lastFailureAt) so escalation across the new window still behaves correctly.

What is the highest-risk area? Sibling cooldown surfaces. `cooldownUntil` (rate_limit etc.) is already capped at 5 min in `calculateAuthProfileCooldownMs`. `blockedUntil` (codex/wham subscription_limit) is driven by upstream `reset_after_seconds` and not arbitrary multi-hour. `disabledUntil` with `reason=auth_permanent` already capped at 60 min default. Only `disabledUntil` with `reason=billing` was producing multi-hour lockouts. `clampStaleBillingDisable` explicitly only touches `disabledReason === "billing"` entries so the other surfaces are unaffected.

How is that risk mitigated? Tests cover sibling stability (the clamp helper explicitly does not touch `auth_permanent`, `cooldownUntil`, or `blockedUntil`). Existing model-fallback and order tests continue to pass.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer review.

Waiting on: nothing from the author at the moment.

Bot/reviewer comments addressed: ClawSweeper P1 (moving-window clamp) — fixed by anchoring `clampStaleBillingDisable` to `lastFailureAt` so the cooldown window cannot slide across fresh persisted-store loads, commit `3918474de2`. See "ClawSweeper P1 fix — moving-window clamp anchored to `lastFailureAt`" above for the anchor fix and the refreshed inline reload-recovery proof. The earlier checked-in `qa/proofs` runtime-fixture artifacts were removed; the proof now lives inline in this PR body.

Still open (separate, maintainer call): the shortened billing-disable defaults (5h/24h → 5m/15m) are a compatibility-sensitive policy decision per root `AGENTS.md` and are not part of this bug fix.


---

## Docs sync (addresses ClawSweeper P1 "configuration reference still advertises old 5h/24h defaults")

Updated `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md` `auth.cooldowns` section so the public reference matches the tightened runtime defaults shipped here: `billingBackoffHours` default `~5 min` (`0.0833` h) and `billingMaxHours` default `15 min` (`0.25` h), with the JSON5 example, per-provider override example, and prose bullets all updated and a note that the field unit stays hours while the default is intentionally sub-hour. Commit `05719b6c34`. Doc lint clean (`markdownlint-cli2 --config config/markdownlint-cli2.jsonc`: 0 errors).
target.author: sweetcornna

The earlier checked-in `qa/proofs` runtime-fixture artifacts were removed; the proof now lives inline in this PR body.

Still open (separate, maintainer call): the shortened billing-disable defaults (5h/24h → 5m/15m) are a compatibility-sensitive policy decision per root `AGENTS.md` and are not part of this bug fix.
